thread.section_title: Murrieta, CA Local News and More | NewsBreak

author: NewsBreak Original
text:
By Mathew Richards ,
2 hours ago RIVERSIDE, California — A 57-year-old Riverside man has been sentenced to 15 years in federal prison for his role in a methamphetamine trafficking operation, the Justice Department announced. Javier Rodriguez, a member of the Casa Blanca Rifa street gang, pleaded guilty to multiple felonies related to drug distribution and money laundering.
Rodriguez was sentenced by United States District Judge Jesus G. Bernal after admitting to eight felonies, including conspiracy to distribute and import methamphetamine, possession of methamphetamine for distribution, being a felon in possession of ammunition, and attempted international promotional money laundering. He has been in federal custody since his guilty plea on March 18.
The investigation, which targeted the Casa Blanca Rifa gang's criminal activities, revealed that from April to August 2020, Rodriguez and others purchased large quantities of methamphetamine from suppliers in Mexico, Los Angeles, and Riverside counties. The drugs were then distributed throughout the Inland Empire, with Rodriguez allegedly trafficking from his mother's home, according to the Justice Department.
During the investigation, law enforcement seized over 150 pounds of methamphetamine and $31,035 in cash. In May 2020, Rodriguez was found in possession of 150 rounds of ammunition, despite being legally prohibited due to six prior felony convictions in Riverside and Los Angeles counties.
Rodriguez is the last of 16 defendants to be sentenced in this case. The lead defendant, 51-year-old Timoteo Gomez, also of Riverside, received a 21-year sentence in May for his involvement in the conspiracy. Other defendants received sentences ranging from two to 12 years.
The FBI’s Inland Empire Safe Streets Task Force, a joint federal and state gang task force, led the investigation with assistance from multiple agencies, including the Drug Enforcement Administration, IRS Criminal Investigation, and the Riverside County Sheriff’s Department. Assistant United States Attorney Eli A. Alcaraz prosecuted the case.
